intro: Zanthoxylum becquetii, also known as the Baka pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, native to Cameroon. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from pain, inflammation, and fever, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum becquetii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of essential oils and oleoresins.
